mission.title: flux: unable to leverage chart version as label with `OCIRepository`
mission.description: unable to leverage chart version as label with `OCIRepository`. This issue affects 5+ users.

steps.title: Identify flux troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your flux deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux -n flux --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review flux configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant flux configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux
kubectl get configmap -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=flux
```
### Describe the bug

When applying an helm chart from an oci repository, chart version appended with a suffix leading to edge cases on some charts.

steps.title: Apply the fix for unable to leverage chart version as label with
steps.description:
Hmm they should've used AppVersion like Helm recommends: https://github.com/actions/actions-runner-controller/blob/a152741a1a6afa992f8d836a029d551984149c8f/charts/gha-runner-scale-set/templates/_helpers.tpl#L41

The workaround is to replace the label value:

```yaml
apiVersion:
```yaml
apiVersion: source.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v1beta2
kind: OCIRepository
metadata:
  name: arc
  # namespace: default
spec:
  interval: 10m
  layerSelector:
    mediaType: "application/vnd.cncf.helm.chart.content.v1.tar+gzip"
    operation: copy
  url: oci://ghcr.io/actions/actions-runner-controller-charts/gha-runner-scale-set-controller
  ref:
    semver: "0.9.3"
---
apiVersion: helm.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v2
kind: HelmRelease
metadata:
  name: arc
spec:
  interval: 1m0s
  chartRef:
    kind: OCIRepository
    name: arc
  values:
    githubConfigSecret: pre-defined-secret
    serviceAccount:
      name: "
```

steps.title: Upgrade flux to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade flux:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade flux flux/flux --namespace flux
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n flux
helm list -n flux
```

steps.title: Confirm unable to leverage chart version as label with is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux -n flux --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n flux --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
Hmm they should've used AppVersion like Helm recommends: https://github.com/actions/actions-runner-controller/blob/a152741a1a6afa992f8d836a029d551984149c8f/charts/gha-runner-scale-set/templates/_helpers.tpl#L41

The workaround is to replace the label value:

```yaml
apiVersion: helm.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v2
kind: HelmRelease
spec:
  postRenderers:
    - kustomize:
        patches:
          -
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: source.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v1beta2
kind: OCIRepository
metadata:
  name: arc
  # namespace: default
spec:
  interval: 10m
  layerSelector:
    mediaType: "application/vnd.cncf.helm.chart.content.v1.tar+gzip"
    operation: copy
  url: oci://ghcr.io/actions/actions-runner-controller-charts/gha-runner-scale-set-controller
  ref:
    semver: "0.9.3"
---
apiVersion: helm.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v2
kind: HelmRelease
metadata:
  name: arc
spec:
  interval: 1m0s
  chartRef:
    kind: OCIRepository
    name: arc
  values:
    githubConfigSecret: pre-defined-secret
    serviceAccount:
      name: "service-account-arc"

apiVersion: helm.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v2
kind: HelmRelease
spec:
  postRenderers:
    - kustomize:
        patches:
          - target:
              kind: Deployment
            patch: |
              - op: replace
                path: /spec/template/metadata/labels/app.kubernetes.io~1version
                value: latest

❯ helm get all dagger
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: dagger
    app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: Helm
    app.kubernetes.io/name: dagger-helm
    app.kubernetes.io/part-of: dagger-helm
    app.kubernetes.io/version: v0.13.5+a076bb44441a
    helm.sh/chart: dagger-helm-0.13.5_a076bb44441a
    helm.toolkit.fluxcd.io/name: dagger
    helm.toolkit.fluxcd.io/namespace: xxx
  name: dagger-dagger-helm-engine
  namespace: xxx

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with flux installed or the issue environment reproducible.
